name: Goujian
description: Goujian, also known as King Goujian of Yue, was the ruler of the Kingdom of Yue during the late Spring and Autumn period of Chinese history (771 to 476 BCE). He is a prominent figure celebrated for his perseverance and eventual triumph over adversity. After being defeated by King Fuchai of Wu, Goujian endured years of humiliation and servitude, feigning loyalty while secretly plotting his revenge. He famously tasted bitter bile daily to remind himself of his humiliation and strengthen his resolve. He employed skilled advisors like Fan Li and Wen Zhong, who helped him rebuild his kingdom's strength through economic reforms, military training, and strategic alliances. After decades of meticulous planning, Goujian launched a successful campaign against Wu, ultimately defeating and executing Fuchai. Goujian's story serves as a powerful moral lesson about the importance of patience, resilience, and strategic thinking in overcoming seemingly insurmountable challenges. His reign marked a period of prosperity and expansion for Yue, solidifying his legacy as one of the most influential rulers of the Spring and Autumn period, and a symbol of unwavering determination in Chinese culture. His story is often cited as an example of the proverb 'Sleeping on brushwood and tasting gall', representing enduring hardship to achieve a goal. He is remembered for his ability to swallow his pride and endure hardship to achieve his ultimate goal of revenge and restoring his kingdom.
subtitle: The King Who Endured and Conquered
achievements: Rebuilding the Kingdom of Yue after defeat
Defeating and executing King Fuchai of Wu
Implementing economic reforms that strengthened Yue
Forging strategic alliances to isolate Wu
Symbol of perseverance and resilience in Chinese culture

keyMyths.title: The Tasting of Bitter Bile
keyMyths.description: Goujian tasted bitter bile daily to remind himself of his humiliation and fuel his determination for revenge against Fuchai. This act symbolizes his unwavering commitment to his goal. It demonstrates his ability to endure unpleasantness for a greater purpose.

keyMyths.title: The Sleeping on Brushwood
keyMyths.description: Goujian slept on brushwood to remind himself of the hardships endured by his people and the loss of his kingdom. This served as a constant reminder of his duty to restore Yue to its former glory. It's a symbol of his empathy and dedication.

keyMyths.title: The Strategic Counsel of Fan Li
keyMyths.description: Fan Li's wise counsel and strategic planning were instrumental in Goujian's eventual victory over Wu. He advised Goujian on economic reforms, military training, and diplomatic maneuvers. Fan Li's loyalty and ingenuity were crucial to Yue's resurgence.

sources.author: Sima Qian
sources.description: A comprehensive historical account of ancient China, including the story of Goujian and his conflict with Fuchai.
